claim: 17 uses of 'clitic' for person markers — review each against the corrected doctrine: = is the Nakagawa-lineage orthographic convention (nakagawa2024:58-60), NOT a clitic analysis; person markers are morphologically mixed (=an clitic-like, prefixal set affix-like). Reword unfounded assertions; keep defensible separability discussions.
evidence: user correction 2026-07-04, verified against nakagawa2024:58-60 (OCR files 56-58), sato2008 (ku-kor wa k-ek), ijas2023 (428 hyphen vs 2 =)
proposed_edit: systematic renotation i=→i-, u=→u-, ko=/e= applicatives → hyphen; clitic→prefix terminology
